This family consists of around 140 species, including trees, shrubs, and vines, found primarily in tropical and subtropical regions. Maropsis deserti is native to the Mediterranean region, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its environment. The Sapindaceae family is part of the larger order Sapindales, which also includes other plant families such as Anacardiaceae and Burseraceae. By studying Maropsis deserti's classification, researchers can gain insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
